items.title: Jamaica braces for Category 4 hurricane | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The US National Hurricane Center is warning Hurricane Melissa could bring life-threatening flooding and landslides, as it moves closer toward the Caribbean island of Jamaica.  

Melissa is forecast to intensify rapidly in the coming hours, and could reach Category 4 strength as it moves over warmer waters.  The hurricane is expected to turn northwards and hit Jamaica in the coming days.  It has already been blamed for several deaths in Haiti and the Dominican Republic.  The slow-moving system is expected to dump massive amounts of rain in Jamaica.  

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Ivory Coast elections: President Ouattara bans opponents from running, faces election boycotts
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Polls have closed in Ivory Coast's presidential election. Incumbant President Alassane Ouattara appears poised to win a fourth term in office.
There have been protests over the exclusion of key challengers. The election comes amid heightened security following past violence triggered by election feuds. Critics say blacklisting opponents has given Ouattara an unfair advantage.

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 Polls close in Ivory Coast elections
0:29 DW West Africa Correspondent Azeezat Olaoluwa reports fon why voter turnout has been low

items.title: Europe's industrial future: Can it break free from China? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Global supply chains are being weaponized - that's the warning coming from the European Commission. DW's chief political editor Michaela Küfner speaks with Lars-Hendrik Röller, former economic advisor to former chancellor Angela Merkel and founder and chair of the Berlin Global Dialogue, about Germany's and the EU's economic dependencies on China. What should the next step be amid rising economic tensions, and what should the EU’s strategy look like. 

#chinatrade #rareearths #germancars

items.title: Why Trump's trade talks are dominating this year's ASEAN summit agenda in Malaysia | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Malaysia's Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim is hosting dozens of leaders and heads of state for the summit of Southeast Asian nations. Ibrahim is also welcoming leaders from Latin America and Africa - but all eyes will be on US President Donald Trump, who is en route to Kuala Lumpur. With his tariffs disrupting global trade, many leaders are eager to negotiate with him. Trump is also looking to bolster his image as a peacemaker in a long-running dispute between Cambodia and Thailand.

Chapter Breakdown:
0:00 Malaysia's prime minister welcomes leaders to Kuala Lumpur
0:39 What is ASEAN and why is this year's summit so important?
2:39 Georg Matthes, DW's Asia-Pacific Bureau Chief, on what's at stake at the ASEAN summit

items.title: 'This is a major crisis:' Why Porsche puts the brakes on EVs | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Profits at German carmaker Porsche plunged by nearly 96 percent in the first nine months of this year. Porsche said the drop in profits was due to a change in strategy as the company shifted its focus back to combustion engines after weak demand for its electric vehicles. 

Chapter Breakdown

items.title: Ukraine coalition: A pledge to take Russian oil and gas off global markets? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Ukraine's allies have pledged to take Russian oil and gas off the global market as they try to disrupt Moscow's biggest source of revenue. The leaders of Britain and Ukraine held talks with the so-called Coalition of the Willing in London. They also discussed using billions of frozen Russian assets to bolster Ukraine's defenses.

items.title: Why support for Argentina's Milei is waning | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Argentina is preparing to vote in a mid-term election test on Sunday for President Javier Milei’s radical free-market experiment. Since taking office he's cut spending and slashed inflation. But scandals and austerity have fueled discontent. And then there's the added complication of Milei's ally Donald Trump, who has tied US aid to Milei's success at the ballot box. Young people, who had catapulted him to power in 2023, are now divided between support, disillusionment, and a loss of enthusiasm following widespread adjustments ranging from universities to disability benefits.

#Argentina #JavierMilei #ArgetinaElection
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: How far is the US willing to go in its conflict with Colombia and Venezuela? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The Trump administration leveled sanctions against leftist leader Gustavo Petro over drug trade allegations. It comes as the US military escalates a campaign against alleged drug-smuggling vessels in the Caribbean Sea.

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on Friday announced the imposition of US sanctions on Colombian President Gustavo Petro. The US accuses Petro of failing to stop drug production in the country. His wife and eldest son were sanctioned with him, as was Interior Minister Armando Benedetti. "President Petro has allowed drug cartels to flourish and refused to stop this activity," said Bessent. "President Trump is taking strong action to protect our nation and make clear that we will not tolerate the trafficking of drugs into our nation." It is the latest escalation in US-Colombian relations as US President Donald Trump unleashes the US military in the hemisphere; deploying troops to target purported drug cartels, as well as threatening invasions and covert operations in Latin America. Colombia's President Petro has accused the US of ignoring international law to murder innocent people in its drug war.

Pentagon deploys advanced aircraft carrier to Caribbean
US President Donald Trump has redeployed a significant amount of military hardware to the waters of Latin America and the Caribbean for his drug interdiction scheme. Beyond bombers and warships deployed to the region, the Pentagon announced on Friday that it has dispatched the world’s largest aircraft carrier, USS Gerald R. Ford, and its carrier air wing to the Caribbean. Chief Pentagon spokesperson Sean Parnell said Friday that the presence of the USS Gerald R. Ford in the region will "bolster U.S. capacity to detect, monitor, and disrupt illicit actors and activities that compromise the safety and prosperity of the United States homeland and our security in the Western Hemisphere. These forces will enhance and augment existing capabilities to disrupt narcotics trafficking and degrade and dismantle TCOs [Transnational Criminal Organizations]," he added. Pentagon carries out more strikes against alleged drug-smuggling vessels Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth also announced Friday the 10th US strike on suspected drug traffickers in the region. He said six people had been killed in the operation, bringing to 43 the total number of individuals killed by US forces since the targeted drug engagements began in September.

#Colombia #Caribbean #USSFord

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Trump cancels Canada trade talks over Ontario ad featuring Reagan | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Donald Trump canceled trade talks with Canada after an ad from the government of Ontario used remarks by former President Ronald Reagan opposing tariffs. Trump called the ad fake and said it could affect an upcoming US court decision on the legality of tariffs.

items.title: What is preventing progress in heated land negotiations between Thailand and Cambodia? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Tensions have risen at the Cambodia–Thailand border over disputed land in Ban Prey Chan village. Cambodian homes now lie within Thai-marked territory, and some residents have been forced to leave their homes or lost their jobs. Locals have called for peace while ASEAN observers have monitored ceasefire violations, concerned about another outbreak of regional instability.
